title: STEM2Crystal-Bench
subtitle: Microscopy-guided crystal structure reconstruction

synthetic.name: Synthetic benchmark
synthetic.description: 1021-structure test set, three controlled noise regimes (Table 1).

real5.name: Real-5 (real STEM monolayers)
real5.description: 5 genuine 2D-monolayer real STEM images with single-layer ground truth (Table 2). Zero-shot, no templating.

sccd.name: SCCD
sccd.full_name: From Noisy STEM to Crystal Structure: Evidence-Structure CoDiffusion under Composition Constraints
sccd.summary: Two coupled diffusion branches — one for image evidence, one for the crystal — that exchange information in both directions.
sccd.description: SCCD separates the problem into two parts and solves them together. An evidence-diffusion branch denoises a structure-aligned, mask-like evidence map from the noisy STEM image. A crystal-diffusion branch, following DiffCSP, denoises the lattice and the per-atom fractional coordinates under the given composition. The two branches exchange a global feature in both directions at every diffusion step: the structure feature constrains evidence denoising, and an image feature (added by FiLM-style modulation) gives the structure branch noise-robust geometric cues. Training runs in three phases — an evidence denoiser, a composition-only structure model, then the joint co-diffusion. On the synthetic benchmark SCCD has the best value on each metric at every noise level; on Real-5 its RMSD is about ten times lower than the composition-only baselines.
highlights: Image evidence and structure are co-diffused in both directions, not mapped in one shot.
Best Hit@1, Hit@5, Match_abs, SG-Acc and lattice calibration at every noise level.
On Real-5, about 10x lower RMSD than DiffCSP and the only method with non-zero SG-Acc.

diffcsp.name: DiffCSP
diffcsp.summary: Equivariant diffusion that generates the lattice and coordinates from a composition. Does not use the image.
diffcsp.description: DiffCSP predicts a crystal by joint equivariant diffusion: given a composition it denoises the lattice and the fractional coordinates together with a periodic, SE(3)-equivariant network. It does not use the STEM image, so it often finds a matching structure within a few samples but gets the absolute lattice scale wrong on this benchmark.
diffcsp.figure_note: Figure 2 from the DiffCSP paper (Jiao et al., NeurIPS 2023).

microscopygpt.name: MicroscopyGPT
microscopygpt.summary: A fine-tuned vision-language model that reads the image and a composition prompt and writes the structure as text.
microscopygpt.description: MicroscopyGPT fine-tunes an 11B vision-language model (LLaMA-3.2 Vision, via LoRA). It reads the STEM image and a composition prompt and writes the structure as text (lattice parameters, species, coordinates), which is then parsed into a CIF. Because the output is a token sequence it can contain format or geometry errors, and it is fairly stable across noise levels.
microscopygpt.figure_note: Figure 1 from the MicroscopyGPT paper (Choudhary et al., JPCL 2025).

mattergen.name: MatterGen
mattergen.summary: A large de-novo diffusion model; samples crystals by chemical system, then we keep formula matches.
mattergen.description: MatterGen is a large de-novo diffusion model for inorganic crystals. It samples from its prior conditioned on a chemical system rather than an exact formula, and does not use the image. For this benchmark we keep the samples whose reduced formula matches the target, so its formula-level hit rate is low.
mattergen.figure_note: Figure 1 from the MatterGen paper (Zeni et al., Nature 2025).

automat.name: AutoMat
automat.summary: An agentic pipeline that denoises the image, retrieves a template, and reconstructs under symmetry constraints.
automat.description: AutoMat is an agentic pipeline that denoises the image, retrieves a template from a library of simulated STEM projections, and reconstructs the structure under symmetry constraints. It works well when a suitable template exists, but its library does not cover the Real-5 monolayers, so it returns no match there.
automat.figure_note: Figure 3 from the AutoMat paper (Yang et al., 2025).

citation.title: From Noisy STEM to Crystal Structure: Evidence-Structure CoDiffusion under Composition Constraints

figure_credit: Method architecture figures are reproduced from each method's paper for academic comparison; © their respective authors.
